---
title: "UI_R023: Language Fallback Chain"
subtitle: "Defines the fallback behavior when translations are missing for the requested language"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "rule"
number: "UI_R023"
date-created: "2025-12-26"
date-modified: "2025-12-26"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "UI Components Law"
article-number:
  number: 23
  title: "Language Fallback Chain"
related_to:
  - "UI_P012: Multi-Language UI Support"
  - "UI_R022: UI Text Translation Pattern"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 23: Language Fallback Chain {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 23 (Language Fallback Chain)**

This rule defines the fallback behavior when a translation is not available in the requested language. The system must gracefully degrade to related languages and ultimately to English, ensuring the UI never displays empty or broken text.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
When a translation is missing, fall back through the chain: zh_CN -> zh_TW -> en. Never display empty text or cause errors due to missing translations.
:::

# Section 1: Fallback Chain Definition {#fallback-definition}

## Subsection 1: Language Fallback Order {#fallback-order}

**Fallback Priority:**

| Requested | Fallback 1 | Fallback 2 | Final |
|-----------|------------|------------|-------|
| zh_CN     | zh_TW      | en_us      | Original text |
| zh_TW     | -          | en_us      | Original text |
| en_us     | -          | -          | Original text |

**Rationale:**

- zh_CN (Simplified Chinese) can fall back to zh_TW (Traditional Chinese) because they share the same meaning with different character forms
- All languages ultimately fall back to English (en_us) as the primary key
- If English key itself is not in dictionary, return the original text unchanged

## Subsection 2: Implementation {#implementation}

**Fallback Logic in Translation Function:**

```r
create_translate_function <- function(dictionary, target_lang) {

  function(text) {
    if (is.null(text) || !nzchar(text)) return(text)

    # Try to find in dictionary
    idx <- match(text, dictionary$en_us)

    # Not in dictionary - return original
    if (is.na(idx)) return(text)

    # Get translation for target language
    translation <- dictionary[[target_lang]][idx]

    # Fallback chain for zh_CN
    if (target_lang == "zh_cn" &&
        (is.na(translation) || !nzchar(translation))) {
      translation <- dictionary$zh_tw[idx]
    }

    # Final fallback to English (original text)
    if (is.na(translation) || !nzchar(translation)) {
      return(text)
    }

    translation
  }
}
```

# Section 2: Graceful Degradation {#graceful-degradation}

## Subsection 1: System Unavailability {#system-unavailable}

**When Translation System is Unavailable:**

```r
initialize_ui_translation <- function() {

  # Check for required dependencies
  if (!exists("app_configs") || is.null(app_configs$language)) {
    message("[Translation] app_configs not available, using identity")
    return(identity)
  }

  # Check for dictionary file
  dict_path <- get_dictionary_path()
  if (!file.exists(dict_path)) {
    message("[Translation] Dictionary file not found: ", dict_path)
    return(identity)
  }

  # Try to read dictionary
  tryCatch({
    dict <- read.csv(dict_path, fileEncoding = "UTF-8-BOM",
                     stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    create_translate_function(dict, get_target_language())
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("[Translation] Error reading dictionary: ", e$message)
    identity
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: Empty Translation Handling {#empty-handling}

**Empty Value Detection:**

```r
# Empty values in CSV are treated as "not translated"
# The following are all considered empty:
# - NA
# - ""
# - "   " (whitespace only)

is_empty_translation <- function(value) {
  is.na(value) || !nzchar(trimws(value))
}
```

**Example Dictionary with Gaps:**

```csv
en_us,zh_tw,zh_cn
Submit,送出,提交
Cancel,取消,取消
Loading,載入中,
Advanced Settings,進階設定,
```

In this example:
- "Loading" in zh_CN will fall back to "載入中" (zh_TW)
- "Advanced Settings" in zh_CN will fall back to "進階設定" (zh_TW)

# Section 3: Error Prevention {#error-prevention}

## Subsection 1: Never Fail Silently {#never-fail}

**Logging Pattern:**

```r
translate <- function(text) {
  translation <- lookup(text)

  # Log when fallback is used (debug mode only)
  if (getOption("mamba.debug", FALSE)) {
    if (is.null(translation) || !nzchar(translation)) {
      message(sprintf("[Translation] Missing: '%s' in %s",
                     text, target_lang))
    }
  }

  # Always return something usable
  if (is.null(translation) || !nzchar(translation)) {
    return(text)
  }
  translation
}
```

## Subsection 2: Startup Validation {#startup-validation}

**Optional Dictionary Validation:**

```r
validate_dictionary <- function(dict) {
  issues <- list()

  # Check for duplicate keys
  dupes <- dict$en_us[duplicated(dict$en_us)]
  if (length(dupes) > 0) {
    issues$duplicates <- dupes
    message("[Translation] Warning: Duplicate keys found: ",
            paste(dupes, collapse = ", "))
  }

  # Check for empty primary keys
  empty_keys <- which(is.na(dict$en_us) | !nzchar(dict$en_us))
  if (length(empty_keys) > 0) {
    issues$empty_keys <- empty_keys
    message("[Translation] Warning: Empty keys at rows: ",
            paste(empty_keys, collapse = ", "))
  }

  invisible(issues)
}
```

# Section 4: Testing Patterns {#testing-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Fallback Testing {#fallback-testing}

**Test Cases:**

```r
test_fallback_chain <- function() {
  # Setup test dictionary
  test_dict <- data.frame(
    en_us = c("Hello", "Goodbye", "Submit"),
    zh_tw = c("你好", "再見", "送出"),
    zh_cn = c("你好", "", ""),  # Empty means fallback
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Test zh_CN with fallback
  translate_cn <- create_translate_function(test_dict, "zh_cn")

  stopifnot(translate_cn("Hello") == "你好")      # Has zh_CN

  stopifnot(translate_cn("Goodbye") == "再見")    # Falls back to zh_TW
  stopifnot(translate_cn("Submit") == "送出")     # Falls back to zh_TW
  stopifnot(translate_cn("Unknown") == "Unknown") # Not in dict

  message("All fallback tests passed")
}
```

# Section 5: Violations and Compliance {#violations-compliance}

## Subsection 1: Violations {#violations}

**Common Violations:**

```r
# VIOLATION: Throwing error on missing translation
if (is.null(translation)) {
  stop("Translation not found!")  # Never throw
}

# VIOLATION: Returning empty string
if (!nzchar(translation)) {
  return("")  # Never return empty
}

# VIOLATION: No fallback chain
translation <- dict$zh_cn[idx]
return(translation)  # Missing fallback to zh_TW
```

## Subsection 2: Compliant Examples {#compliant}

**Correct Implementation:**

```r
# COMPLIANT: Proper fallback chain
translation <- dict$zh_cn[idx]
if (is_empty_translation(translation)) {
  translation <- dict$zh_tw[idx]  # Fallback to zh_TW
}
if (is_empty_translation(translation)) {
  return(text)  # Fallback to original English
}
return(translation)

# COMPLIANT: Identity function as safe default
translate <- identity  # Safe before initialization

# COMPLIANT: Graceful handling
tryCatch(
  initialize_ui_translation(),
  error = function(e) identity
)
```

# Section 6: Principle Relationships {#principle-relationships}

This rule:

1. **Implements UI_P012 (Multi-Language UI Support)** fallback behavior
2. **Works With UI_R022 (UI Text Translation Pattern)** for initialization
3. **Ensures UI_P001 (Natural Language UI)** by never showing broken text

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 23*
*Implements: [UI_P012 Multi-Language UI Support](../principles/UI_P012_multi_language_ui_support.qmd)*
